notes: Registry BUILD classification is wrong — no application code was written. Session migrated the Ralphy skill from global ~/.claude/skills/ralphy into the appydave-plugins repo (making the plugin the single source of truth), then did a brain-sync audit. Produced commits v1.6.0 and v1.6.1. Direct predecessor to session d363ca82 (findings-w3-10-plugins.md) which did the design overhaul. Key signals: Claude hallucinated that the skill didn't exist (user confronted with 'BULSHIT'), voice-dictation noise in prompts, and a closing prompt explicitly asking about knowledge-decay ('any updates to brains that didn't get into the plugin') — a direct statement of AngelEye's purpose.
